name: Allard, Clarence L.
description: With the class of 1924, came a little "snowball" from the slopes of Mount Washington, who had bigger aspirations than ever a snowball had before. "Demi," which is the very appropriate name given to this innocent little boy, had the ambition to become a Chemist, and although sometimes discouraged when he sees the empty benches in the Chem. Lab. at the beginning of each term, he is still struggling manfully towards his goal. He is also prominent on the campus as being president of the well-known organization, The A. T. B. Club. In company with his A. T. B. brother, "Doc," he has traveled over three thousand miles to see the football team play their various opponents. Perhaps there are various underclassmen who will remember "Demi" as being the distributor of those well-known Gym. tickets.

name: Anderson, Paul Howard
motto: "Swede, otherwise known as Theory, Sleeps all day, yet he is always weary."
description: Paul proved his worth by fighting his way out of Berlin, only to face the fight for a B. S. degree. His ambition, however, is to revise the football rules in such a way that the backfield men will have a minimum of thirty seconds to apply his theory for drop-kicking. "Andy" wore pajamas one night instead of his nighty, but this thrilling act cannot be compared to his first ride on an escalator.

name: Avery, Helen Bethana
motto: "Cheeks that shame the Rose."
description: Helen's marked ability in Home Economics seems to indicate the path which she so sedately treads toward domesticity. This progress is interrupted only by two things—telephone calls from "the house on the hill," and the numerous demands that her room-mates make upon her capability, for what they can't do, Helen always can. Her favorite form of recreation is her trips by way of "Rollin's Ford".

name: Dean Proctor Avery
motto: I'm not very big, but I sure am fast.
description: As a member of the "Campus Deans", "Mo" has won fame as a drum-stick artist. He cuts a prominent figure in the neighboring towns of Dover, Newmarket, and Lee Hill. His mail-box is always filled with pink letters, from almost any place from the hills of Hanover to Boston-Town. "Dinny's" name was entered in the two-mile ski race, but he decided to let "Ed" Aulis uphold the fair name of Hanover. "Mo" holds the record for having spent more week-ends out of town than any other three men in college.

name: Doris A. Bachelder
motto: Sh! I shall have to give you a black mark.
description: When the doorbell at the Commons rings twice very fervently, then is heard a great commotion, and suddenly Doris darts for the door, and off they go — where? No one knows. Doris makes a worthy proctor. Whenever her room-mate gets noisy, she betakes herself to the Library and then imbibes much History and Zoology. She pursues her studies diligently; nevertheless she never neglects her social duties, and at every hilarious meeting of the Commons gang, Doris is very much present.

name: Leslie Randolph Bacon
motto: Men take each other's measure when they meet for the first time — and every time they meet.
description: Although "Bake" comes from the little town of Henniker, he has succeeded in putting his town on the map by capturing scholastic honors at N. H. C. He was the only boy in his class while in high school, so maybe that is why he won't give the co-eds any time. However, it is rumored that he has an eye on a certain little Freshman. (Enuff Sed.) They say the King likes bees, that's why "Bake" has become a "drone". His pet hobby is chasing molecules. Of course, he has his good points as well as bad ones, and we feel sure, that as a conscientious worker, he will capture the King's crown some day.

name: Baker, Elizabeth
motto: If she's a college girl with heart awhirl And lots of pep Happy, snappy, lucky, plucky, Then I'll bet You mean "Tab" Baker!
description: "Tab" is known among her many friends as a good sport, always ready for excitement. Her impulsiveness has led to many thrilling experiences, and when "Tab" appears to tell you of the last one, or about her latest crush, you may be sure of a good laugh, but "Tab's" will be heard above all the others. You can locate "Tab" by her laugh, even when the joke is on her. Her college career has been a round of activities, ranging from interpretive dancing to teaching a Sunday School class. How she manages to accomplish so much in one short day, makes us all wonder.

name: Ball, George H.
motto: Men say that George is ambitious.
description: Quite true with this honorable gentleman! He may not be a social giant, but when it comes to deep stuff, he is a mental prodigy. Money and Banking, Business Law, and Accounting will serve him when some day he becomes a M. B. S. George not only has a mind for studies, but when it comes to naming the co-eds he is still a leader. Informals tempt him only when he has finished his Monday's assignment in Accounting. Believing in a dual personality, he has a good start in the development of both. When he gets his major in Economics, he will be satisfied with one. The other is more tragic. After graduation from the Glee Club, he intends to take a basic course in tenor at the Boston Conservatory of Music.

name: Ballantyne, Gordon Robert
motto: If I ever pass that final.
description: Allow us to present Professor Babcock's prodigy; "Bal", with his never-failing smile. He has never been known to get less than 99.9% in his subjects since he matriculated at this institution, with one exception, when in his Junior year he slipped and drew a pass for 98% in English History. Doubtless this very radical slip was due to the fact that things were not "normal" (Posse). Closer observation of this singularly handsome student reveals that his ability is not restricted to scholastic activities. He may be found every morning carefully nursing the pool balls in his spare time, and report has it, that he wields a wicked cue. And when he is not engaged at this pastime, his sonorous voice softly sings, "Just one more waltz with you, dear", "Milly knows".

name: Lucas, E. Bannon
motto: Possession is eleven points in the law.
clubs: Officers Club, R. O. T. C. (3)
description: "Sh-h-h-h! a confidential man arrives." "Luke" has all the "inside dope" on the campus and can tell you CONFIDENTIALLY anything from what "Prexy" does or had for breakfast, to the day when the college will be called a university. "Luke's" affairs de coeur were numerous until he finally found a suitable governess. Never mind, old top, tell us about it. In spite of his shortcomings, he is generous to a fault, and his heart is in the right place. "Have any of you fellows got——"

name: Bartlett, Dorothy Frances
motto: Parle-vous français? Sprechen Sie deutsch? Hable Usted español?
description: From "Dot's" childlike features and peaches-and-cream complexion one might infer that——, but we promised not to mention men. Her giggles and laughter can be heard resounding through the Commons building, bringing forth some wary proctor. Next to her Math. book, "Dot" prefers her trusty hockey stick. As a linguist she has us all beaten, because she can express her opinions in at least four different languages. But this isn't all! Few nights go by without finding "Dot" dancing the "light fantastic" in the Commons parlor.

name: Bartlett, Henry Hadley
description: "Hen" blew into Durham from Bible Hill, with the class of 1923. He served as understudy to Dean Pettee for two years, holding such important positions as Superintendent of the Durham Waterworks, and valet to the Dean's old horse. After serving there for twenty-four long and dreary months, he decided to take a forward step into public life and became one of Dube's "hash slingers". Until this year, Henry has been quite devoted to his studies, but we are afraid that he has slipped at last, as he has been seen calling at a certain Professor's house on Madbury Road.

name: Berry, Kenneth
description: "Ken" was an original '23 man, but at the beginning of his Junior year he decided to make excelsior on account of his father's ill health, so now he belongs to the great and glorious class of 1924. We wonder if he remembers an afternoon spent in a certain Professor's parlor on Madbury Road in very pleasant company. Although not required to take Physical Education, "Ken" devoted many hours to that subject from choice. Kenneth has been very strict about leaving the co-eds alone, but we are wondering what he does back home. A better chum than "Ken" is hard to find, and he has many friends among his classmates.

name: Boisclair, Herman Harry
description: "Bo" once took the Valentine-Smith Scholarship examination and when he didn't win it he laid it to the fact that one can't have hair and brains too. He hadn't been in Durham two weeks before he incurred the hatred of Sneaker Neville because he broke up a fifteen-cent sale. Sneaker was a sophomore, too. Hermie's accomplishments include great proficiency at ducking pillows and vegetables, knowledge of just what to say and how and when to say it, and popularity with certain Profs., which accounts for numerous week-ends in Manchester. If you think he's slow, take a trip with him on the military "Rolls-Royce". Even certain well-known brave men wouldn't do it again. We all wish "Bo" success in love, and feel sure that as a man of wide experience, he will make the best selection. A Pierce-Arrow and a palace of his own design will soon be waiting for her.

name: Boucher, Kathryn Natalie
motto: "I'll go back on the farm."
description: Kathryn hails from "away up north", which accounts for the fact that she does not mind in the least the walk from Professor Fuller's house to "T" Hall. She is a frequent visitor at the Commons, and entertains the inmates there with her witty remarks. But there is one thing which still remains a mystery to most of us: "Why does she get so enthused over those frequent visits to Boston to visit her aunt (?)?" Do you know?

name: Boulay, George L.
motto: In physique, George is small, But oh, so fond of mouth That you can hear him coming From North, East, West and South.
description: Born on the Fourth of July, the noisiest of all days, George inherited the love for noise. He does not confine his racket to that day alone, however, but to every day — and all night. During his Freshman and Sophomore years, while in Durham, he confined his activities to Ballard Hall, where he took the part of the wildcat's playmate for "Lefty" Rowell. As to his studies during his first and second years, we are somewhat doubtful. We are not sure whether he carried on Extension or Research work in Newmarket and Dover, and he has never told us. In spite of all the noise and mystery that overhangs "the boy's" life, we all find him ready to do his bit in everything he undertakes, and we all wish him the best of success in his life work.

name: Bridges, Webster Easterbrook
motto: Late to bed and late to rise, Makes dark circles under Webbie's eyes.
description: "String" started in as an Engineer, but he soon found that the Forestry Course offered him more opportunities to visit the College Woods, and since changing, he has made lengthy strides in the art of "Bush-whacking". Just because "Bean" was a swimming instructor at one of Concord's most exclusive bathing beaches, and has saved nine lives (i.e., one cat), does not signify that he wears fins—Not much! Nevertheless, it takes a lean horse to win a long race, and we are betting on "String".

name: Briggs, Norman Edward
clubs: Color Sergeant, R. O. T. C. (2), (3)
description: Although a native of Massachusetts, "Normie" spends most of his summers in Vermont, and can tell many thrilling tales of the Green Mountains. He majors in Mil. Art as Color Sergeant, and spends the rest of his time in the Agricultural building. This young man gained fame by running Cross Country his Freshman year, and as a half-miler at the inter-fraternity interclass meet by leading the field by a hundred yards in the first lap garbed in his conspicuous red jersey.

name: Brown, Mary Ella
motto: Absence makes the heart grow fonder.
description: Since Mary started her career by train, She's been an ardent patron of the Boston and Maine. You think she looks demure, so meek and shy, But when you really know her — oh my! In talking, there is none whom she can't beat, Recalling days with "Glenn" and nights with "Pete". Its every course, however hard, she sharks, As we may see by her honor marks. But best of all, she is one of the few, The best of sports and a friend true blue.

name: Burnham, Helen B.
motto: One minute for work and two for fun,
clubs: Executive Committee, N. H. Y. P. O. (3)
description: Helen never misses a chance for a pun. Although in English she has her major, In using slang you cannot beat her. She never slights her studies at all Since for the men, she does not fall, As an artist, she has made a name, Perhaps as a singer she'll win more fame. As for Y. W. C. A. and the church To fill her place you'll have to search.

name: Butler, Philbrook R.
description: This representative of the "Seaport Town" came to U. N. H. in the fall of 1920, to show "us college folks" what they raise on the sea-shore. Due to the fact that he is musically inclined, he signed up at once for the band and orchestra, and has "held fast" ever since. If you don't believe he can push his slide trombone, just ask the Fairchild boys about him.

name: Callahan, Ruth V.
motto: Hurry, hurry up!
description: Sh! yes it's a Zeta Psi pin. No, don't ask her if it means anything - unless you're ready to leave in a hurry. She plays basketball, is an awful Zoo shark, and is a corking good kid. And that story about the well, isn't as deep as you might think.

name: Calpin, Jack Leslie
motto: The oily boid catches the woim, But the woim toined
description: No matter when one is talking with "Jack" about financial circumstances, he'll always say he's just got twelve cents. At the end of his Sophomore year, he found that twelve cents was not sufficient funds with which to graduate. Consequently, he left and worked a year to salt away some "jack," and he is now in the class of '24 with "just twelve cents" - but he doesn't care, - he broke even anyway. "Jack," the foul ball of the Calpin family, is an accomplished musician on any instrument, especially the piano. Paderewski is good, but "Calpin" is better - still. When he was a boy his mother didn't let him play on the piano because she was afraid he would fall off - but you'd ought to see him now - he's wrecking 'em - so much so, that the boys are going to make him a wooden piano with rubber strings.

name: Caulstone, Albert R.
description: "Doc" came with the class of '24, with the great and noble ambition of becoming an artist. He still has the ambition, but has decided to complete his college course before entering the artistic field. He is a hot air artist in the college band, thus still carrying on his artistic work. "Doc" as well as his brother Architects, burns the midnight oil in the office at DeMerritt Hall very often. His hobby is making posters, and no advertising campaign is complete without the touch of his brush. His works of art adorn the walls of many rooms throughout the institution.

name: Choate, Harold Fairbanks
description: Coming in with the class of 1923, "Choatie" decided that things were too strenuous, so he dropped into the folds of 1924. Although in the Engineering course, Harold is majoring in the study of the fair sex, and is well known in surrounding towns which he used to visit in company with "Les" and his well known Ford. He is especially devoted to his Math. Courses, having been known to take the same course several times. Because of the condition of his heart, Harold was excused from Mil. Art and Phys. Ed., but he went out for class football his Sophomore year. He was once a famous candy merchant in the Barracks, building up a fine trade which he sold out to "Ponzi" Huckins, to take a position as steward in Grant's café.

name: Cleaves, Chester Freeman
motto: A brand from the burning.
description: "Chet" transferred from Bates because he thought New Hampshire a better college, and first made himself conspicuous by conducting the one-man orchestra at our new playhouse on the "great white way". He was forced upon our class through the medium of the registration list and the conspiracy of "Dad" Henderson, and we have accepted him into our midst. We don't know much about him as yet, but expect great things along the line of track and baseball. He experiences a financial set-back periodically, due to the week-end trips to Portland, but as "Chet" says "The woman deserves attention."

He was forced upon our class through the medium of the registration list and the conspiracy of "Dad" Henderson, and we have accepted him into our midst. We don't know much about him as yet, but expect great things along the line of track and baseball. He experiences a financial set-back periodically, due to the week-end trips to Portland, but as "Chet" says "The woman deserves attention."

name: Cobleigh, Neal Wayne
motto: For a better friend, look no further.
description: "Gimp" blew down from Nashua to N. H. C., and started tickling the ivories and busting all the banjo strings in Durham. It wasn't long before he represented the society of the Barracks, by blossoming out at one or two social functions in a full "house." As a cub reporter, "Gimp" is there; and besides himself, no one can read his short-hand. It wasn't until the Fall of his Junior year that his class-mates knew that "Charlie" Brickley's understudy was in their midst. As a social butterfly, "Cob" is highly colored (black or white?) take your choice. No event is successful without his presence, and his partner—always the same, pretty brunette. "Gimp" also has a high rank in the movie course, aided and abetted by his little "Dotty."

name: Connor, Leroy Allen
motto: Let's have a game of whist.
description: "Freddie," a nice quiet little chap, came to us from Henniker in the Fall of 1919. After spending a couple of years here, he thought that he had his education and tried working, but that didn't satisfy him very well, so this year he is back with us again. As to what he really plans to do is a mystery. Some think that he has outside activities, as he has only been on the campus two week-ends this fall. Just where he spends his time is another mystery, but some think that "Freddie" is quite interested in the study of Hill and also of Freda. Perhaps it would be a good thing to take up a collection for him, as postage and stationery cost him a small fortune. Already, Fred has a string of bells hitched to some reins, so he can keep track of the rest of the family.

name: Cree, Rachel F.
motto: A health to you and wealth to you And the best that life can give to you, May fortune still be kind to you, and happiness be true to you, And Life be long and good to you is the toast of all your friends to you.
description: "Cree" started in with the class of 1923, but decided to take a vacation and wait for a good class, the result being in our favor. We've never seen "Cree" when she was dignified, but the fact that she has taught school for two whole years, proves to us that she can be at times. Her virtues are many, not the least being that of an expert fudge-maker. Judging from the weekly letter always to be found on Smith Hall table, someone else has discovered this talent, too. If you want to make "Cree" talk, ask her about "God's Country". Some of those big-game stories are hard to believe, but then, we all have our faults, and we love her for a' that.

name: Cronin, John Joseph
motto: A Chemist— but how I can dance.
description: "Jack," a quiet, modest young son of Erin, and to all appearances a woman-hater, came to us from a place that is called Needham, somewhere in Massachusetts. All went well with "Jackie" until he donned a baseball uniform, and acquired a mean arm as a pitcher and a Price raiser. To quote Economics, "The Price did not remain constant." John's baseball ability stands him in good stead as a peanut-thrower in the Franklin. But that isn't all that he can throw; ask the co-eds. His outside activities are hidden within "Yve's" paradise in Manchester. Nuff said.

name: Curtiss, Glenna F.
motto: And the Queen lifted her eyes, and they dwelt languidly.
description: Glenna? Well, "she has a heart - how shall I say - full soon made glad". And she has an atmosphere all of her own. There is an independence - and - I don't care - a - bit, plenty of good looks, loads of good-looking clothes; this year, ear-rings - oh, always ear-rings, and a demand for her dances, that is just ruining Menke's disposition.

name: Cutler, Henry Everton
motto: Bring 'em on, my six big ones.
clubs: First Sergeant, R. O. T. C. (3)
description: "Evy" stepped off the briny deep, and slipped out of his broadcloth to don his "civvies" plus a little blue cap, thus to begin his career at N. H. C. He first showed that big things come in small packages as a halfback on the "Frosh" football team, and later to hold the same on the Varsity squad. As a boxer, he's a champ, his little head proving wild and woolly at times. Above all, his radiant smile and cheerful "Howie", are his main factors; but then, he is a "Keene Boy".

name: Daniell, Robert Lovekin
description: "Bob" originally wanted to go to Dartmouth, but as he thought that place was too rough for him, he cast his lot with the rest of the Franklin gang then entering New Hampshire State. Although his first room-mate left school and joined the Navy, "Bob" still stuck, and became an honor student. He has been seen with many different co-eds on various occasions, but he still remains true to the one who waits for him in Boston, and we can always tell when his tri-weekly letter has been delayed. "Bob" is rather excitable at times as was shown one night when the fire alarm sounded. He grabbed his skull ash tray and rushed out of the house with it. As yet he has not decided whether to become a C. P. A., or a Forester, but we do know that he will make good at whatever he attempts.

name: Davis, Adaline G.
description: When Adaline first arrived at New Hampshire College, she registered as a Home Economics student, but changed her career even before she had even learned to make cocoa without spilling it. Now she is an Arts and Science, but according to her plans when she graduates, we think she would be doing better if she hadn't changed her course at all. Else why are the mails so busy between Northfield and Durham. Her education is going to be complete, because she is taking a very interesting Seminar Course in card playing, and in time, will develop into a shark. Certainly the kind friends who are to aid in this phase of her development are not lacking.

name: Davis, Paul Owen
motto: "Love is an incurable malady."
description: "P.O." comes from the metropolis of the State, the only perfect city in the world. He left his job as an express heaver at $6.00 per., to become a disciple of the "King," and as such he has evolved the nitric acid, ethyl alcohol methods for the ascent upwards to the lower regions. "P.O." calls it the end of a perfect day when the 3:00 o'clock mail brings his bi-daily volume. This correspondence course in theory is followed by laboratory work at Concord during vacations. He very modestly wears the crown of chess and checker championship of Fairchild Hall. To those who know him "P.O." is a quiet, hard working fellow, and a mighty good scout, but with the co-ed's he is an inert element.

name: Dillon, Elvira P.
motto: "You can't judge a book by its cover."
description: Do you know "Elvie" or do you just know Elvira? She is an efficient Y. W. C. A. and class worker and a conscientious student. But she is the kind of girl who is sure to be at all the dances, who spends her Sunday afternoons in the approved manner, and who appreciates the diverse uses of Fords, and can almost drive one. Since "Elvie" became "Scott" free, she enthusiastically "Russells" around.

name: Donnell, Curtis Pierce
motto: If I were as crooked as my hair, I'd be an octopus.
clubs: Battalion Supply Sergeant, R. O. T. C. (2)
First Sergeant, R. O. T. C. (3)
description: Mr. Donnell came to N. H. C. with an ambition to be a chemist, or in other words to fumble empty bottles. But King decided meant Certainly Putrid, and Frosty delivered the K. O. Mrs. Oliver's opinion of him as a chemist is that he is a good dishwiper. When the freshmen howled Curtis fainted, but faint of heart never won fair lady. Since becoming an Arts and Grafter he elected Math. as a major and Physics 6a and 9a as accessories. During the first year, Curtis resided in the Tin Palace, which doubtless accounts for the King's poor judgment. It must be admitted that Curtis does have some ability as a journalist, and that as an all-around fellow he hits about 95%, which entitles him to graduate cum magna laude.

name: Donovan, Patrick Bernard
description: Every morning when the 8:09 from Exeter, Newfields, and way-stations pulls in, Pat is to be seen disembarking with his Geology in one hand and the Boston Post in the other. We all thought him quiet and retiring until one day, he nearly beat-up four of his classmates. But Pat is always there with a helping hand and it is for his good nature and for his willingness to help, that we admire him.

name: Donovan, William Edwin
motto: Well, I'll be—.
description: Wild Bill at the last minute hopped onto a train at Boston and headed for N. H. C., with full intentions of becoming a learned Engineer. After passing a prosperous year at the Barracks, both in studies, cards, and friend-making, he decided to matriculate at Boston College. After spending a strenuous year at this institution, he decided that old New Hampshire was the best place after all, so this year he is back with us, and as wild as ever. When he isn't studying for tests and mid-terms, he spends his time learning the fine points of card playing; and say, can't he pull them in.

name: Dooley, Helen Ward
motto: For she's a jolly good fellow.
description: Some people never do grow up and one of them is "Dooley". She spends so much time studying human nature that there isn't much time left for other scholarly efforts. Does she like sports? Just watch her indoors and out of doors. Although she commutes, "Dad" has charged her three years room rent for the whole of Smith Hall. To make a long story short, she has the faculty of being everywhere and doing everything.

name: Draper, Reuben
motto: He who rides highest has farthest to fall.
description: "Rube" was a confirmed woman hater until he took a Sargent School girl to a dance, and now he is more so, and confines his high-stepping to the track. Because of "Drape's" liking for hot stuff, he has been recently elected to the Durham Fire Department. His training consists of riding to all the football games on the back of a Harley. After all, he is a good-looking fellow when he comes out of the bushes, as we found out after he won a razor on a punch board.

name: Dudley, Alice E.
motto: When does the Sheik come, Dud?
description: "Dud's" sureness gets her into trouble sometimes. If you don't believe it, just ask her, and have all doubt removed. She rather laughs at our efforts at studying, because she goes in for more manly labors, such as wood-shop (furnishing her house), basketball, athletics of all sorts, and specializing in running worn out "Flivvers". She's not a "Chemiker" but she believes in compounds. Her dainty appetite is known by all her friends, that is why they always hide their food.

name: Dunn, H. Irma
motto: Irma's a girl of the sterling sort, Always ready with a witty retort; She's loyal, and loving, and graceful, too— If you don't know her, I'm sorry for you.
description: It is so hard for Irma to wake up in the morning, but we can easily understand why. Conservation of energy certainly must be necessary to make possible the many outbursts of genius and peppery lectures, indiscriminately delivered throughout the day. Perhaps it's just artistic temperament, because Irma has an ambition to be a real "artiste". When we pass her roses over the footlights, we wonder if we will remember Irma and her "little dog".

name: Emerson, Reginald H.
description: This unassuming youth first came to rest in our historic hamlet in the year 1920 when he was an inmate of that notorious institution the "Tin Palace". But he changed from the "meek and mild" to the "wooly and wild" so quickly that even the Palace could not stand the shock and he has since favored Fairchild Hall with his residence. "Doc" came to Durham with the intention of following in the footsteps of his dad's medical career and he has done nobly. But for all this his friends find him a regular fellow and hope that the future will abound in success and happiness.

name: Engel, John N.
motto: He's not a politician, but his habits are good.
description: John seems to take all of his cares lightly enough, but we wonder sometimes, if he has as many as his neighbors next door when they are under the influence of his dear old negro melodies from his banjo. As a prospective member of the A. T. B.'s, Jack ought to be O. K. as his trips to Bates and to Holy Cross signify. So far, he has done quite well as he still holds his own as one of the two remaining "would-be architects" of the class. His latest hobby is studying the "Moons".

name: Fernald, Harold Thompson
motto: Be what you is and not what you aint, because when you aint what you is then you is what you aint.
description: "Kike," with his playmates, was playing tiddledy-winks with sewer-covers in Laconia one day, and got mixed up in fisticuffs, resulting from an argument over a technicality of the game. After the carnage, "Kike" hopped a freight to the next town and there got a job as porter on the Lake Shore. He was fired at Dover so went to Durham and gave himself up to Dean Pettee who sentenced him to four years at hard labor in the Arts and Science course. He says he expects to be liberated in June, 1924, and then he's going to get married - about 1940. At the present he is lying kind of low, because he is afraid the Ku Klux Klan will get him.

motto: Two girls at seventeen are better than one at thirty-four.
description: "Langdon Dewey" - "Baby-Face" - left the City on the Lakes and flivvered down in the "ark" and parked it under the steps of Barracks "B". Besides acquiring a better knowledge of basketball, "Lang" also learned how to get liquid soap from a glass container. During his first year at college the co-eds thought he was a fruit-dealer's son - those old corduroy pants of his would stand alone - "you could lay them on the ground, they'd get up and walk around," etc.

motto: Well, there is yet one day of life before me. And whatsoe'er betide, I will enjoy it.
description: We surely envy "Kitty" and all her joy-rides, too, For she's sincere and witty in whatever she may do. She surely must be smart as we have always seen, For as to natural art she is so very keen. We don't know what to say about her as a friend, For to this very day she's ever glad to lend.

name: Gould, Malcolm P.
motto: God helps those who help themselves.
description: Malcolm started his Freshman year in the cardboard palace and became notorious on the campus as "Dumbell Drew's" room-mate. He aspires to become a Chemist but has evaded the Engineering course because of its simplicity (?). "Gouldie" has found it necessary to provide himself with a green eyeshade because of excessive concentration on the pasteboards. He has been very successful along theatrical lines, being a ticket collector in the local movie house. In the near future, he hopes to do some extensive traveling.

name: Gilmore, Laura Belle
motto: Her voice was ever soft, gentle, and low, An excellent thing in woman.
description: "Laurie" has been much interested in the Agricultural Course during the past two years. This was not a surprise to those who knew her so well in Exeter, for they often noticed her gazing at the Field near her home, and the red "Chick" that always came to her call. Her lack of interest in Dairy Husbandry this year is very noticeable. This year she is an ardent follower of the humanities, spending most of her time writing poetry for "Book and Scroll." By her average in the Arts and Science Courses, her great desire to teach in Wilton will no doubt be gratified.

name: Graupner, Ernest Walter
motto: You'll find no Chinese laundries where the River Shannon flows.
description: While hunting with his pack of "Sooner" dogs, "Chink" wandered into the hamlet of Durham. As the dogs had "sooner lie down than hunt," he decided to acquire the rest of his education within these hunting grounds. During his first year, he made three moves a day — breakfast, dinner, and supper, but since that time he has been prominent in all forms of character sketches, and took a leading part in a show once; he was an usher. His failure to pass Calculus was due to the absence of the capital "D" in the Chinese language, and also because he is a sound sleeper — the sound keeps us awake. If he is not recalled by the Immigration Board, he is in hopes of graduating before his grandchildren enter college.
text:
You'll find no Chinese laundries where the River Shannon flows.
While hunting with his pack of "Sooner" dogs, "Chink" wandered into the hamlet of Durham. As the dogs had "sooner lie down than hunt," he decided to acquire the rest of his education within these hunting grounds. During his first year, he made three moves a day — breakfast, dinner, and supper, but since that time he has been prominent in all forms of character sketches, and took a leading part in a show once; he was an usher. His failure to pass Calculus was due to the absence of the capital "D" in the Chinese language, and also because he is a sound sleeper — the sound keeps us awake. If he is not recalled by the Immigration Board, he is in hopes of graduating before his grandchildren enter college.

name: Eustis, B. Grimes
motto: I've taken my fun where I have found it, I've ranged and I've roved in my time
clubs: Commander, Parnell-Corriveau Post, V. F. W. (3)
description: "Harry" came to us with more medals than he could wear on his uniform blouse, and immediately won more honors on the campus by leading his class to victory in the annual picture fight. He would rather fight than eat, and since he can fight fires to his heart's content without any objections being raised, he has adopted the Durham Fire Department. It is often thought that a co-educational institution is a matrimonial bureau, and evidence seems to point that way. "Harry" missed being a commanding officer, so at the end of his second year, he followed "Duke's" example and got married. He has been prominent in many campus activities and has shown the same resourcefulness that he showed on the battlefield. He is a man to be sought as a friend. "Let's go."

name: Raymond, F. Gunn
description: "Ray" came to college to work for Dube, but found time during his Freshman year to make the relay team. Besides that, he is "Rube's" only rival in the hurdles. "Ray" used to give the co-eds his time but has finally come to realize what a mistake it was, and now confines his attentions to our nearby cities. During his Junior year, "Ray" had to help the Military Department, and now has Lt. McKenney as an understudy.

name: Albert, Raymond Hammersley
motto: He is small, but isn't he cute.
description: At least that is what a certain co-ed thinks, and also those who know him. "Bertie" came to us from the wilds of Needham Heights, and bravely entered the Engineering Division. After one year of Labs. and more Labs. he decided to enter the perils of Dean French's division; much to his later sorrow. He is a versatile youth; being able to participate in all kinds of athletics, from baseball to tiddledy-winks.

name: Hardy, Harry Dudley
motto: Quoth a little co-ed, when walking down the street, Who is that great blond god, with those tremendous feet.
description: The activities of this young man have been diversified. He has done everything except taking "Red" Richardson's Poultry courses - but he has done some personal research work in this line. In 1918 he clogged up the machinery of the S. A. T. C., and now he persists in gumming up the works of the R. O. T. C. He is also clever in the rest of his studies. His ability is acknowledged in other scholastic lines. In athletics, he shows aptitude at football, baseball, checkers, cribbage, and dish-washing. Although admired by all members of the fair sex, he firmly denies his liking for them. Why, then, those trips to Concord, and those daily "business" (?) letters.

name: Hartford, Marjorie Laura
motto: And still they gazed, and still the wonder grew That one small head could carry all she knew.
description: We don't know how much she burns the midnight oil, if at all, but she certainly sharks her courses; her marks are all in the higher altitudes. The only thing she doesn't know is the appearance of a mid-term warning. After fastening onto all the scholastic honors which New Hampshire offers, Marjorie plans to teach Math. Something tells us that the students she has will know Math., if she has anything to say about it. She doesn't study all the time. No, no, Alphonse. She is a commuter.

name: Haubrich, Frederick Rockwell
motto: Still waters run deep.
description: "Freddie" gave both Dartmouth and New Hampshire a try and finally decided on New Hampshire. He is a quiet chap, gives much time to his studies, and very little to the co-eds. Rumor has it that he has a girl in Keene but he never imported her so we don't know for a certainty. He steps out once in a while - if you don't believe it ask him about a certain house party at Portland last year.

name: Hayes, Mabel E.
motto: If you have knowledge, let others light their candles from it.
description: After wavering between Exeter and Durham for a year, Mabel has decided to remain in Durham, for which we are all grateful. She is missed by the commuters whose motto is "When in doubt, ask Mabel". Mabel is a good student, and is especially fond of debating and public speaking.

name: Heller, Samuel Earle
clubs: Assistant Advertising Manager, The New Hampshire (2)
description: "Sam" came to us from the grand and glorious town of Claremont, and to all appearances, we have with us a second Rockefeller. As a hot-air artist, at selling "ads" for The New Hampshire, he has no equal. Before choosing New Hampshire College for his Alma Mater, he acted as manager of a large clothing store "back home", and from this occupation, he came to us to show how real business is carried on. He is always to be found on the campus where there is a "gas attack" going on, and without doubt, can uphold the colors of his side. We wish him all success in whatever he may undertake, when stepping out into this big, big world.

name: Hilberg, F. Chauncey
motto: The rolls made a bank-roll for me.
description: This young "lady" blew in from the wilds of Salem with a carpet-bag in one hand and an umbrella in the other. Plodding his way to "T" Hall, he registered as a Chem. Engineer but after being in "royal Company" for two years he was convinced that the Arts and Chem. is not so bad. His greatest asset is his ability as a baker—good luck to the girl that gets him! We have had to eat his baking for the last two years. He tells us that he can't give the co-eds nor any other girls much time but we notice that he is taking an evening course in Dover. Also, that he is taking advance Mil. Art because no doubt he fits in pretty strong with Mc.(D).

name: Hinckley, Russell Crocker
motto: Catch 'em young, treat 'em rough, and tell 'em nothing.
description: After an extensive tour of several "prep" schools in the East and South, "Bud" came to us from Staunton Military Academy with a record as a drummer boy. After Coach Swasey attempted to make him a marathon runner, he began running around with a certain co-ed. They say that he has a table reserved at the Tea Room each Sunday night, but all he spends is the "evening". He came to New Hampshire with the idea of transferring, but certain circumstances have arisen and have evidently changed his mind. He is a full-fledged member of the Riders A. C. and at the present time is trying hard to decide whether he will take Y. M. C. A. work in Manchester, or become a Geologist.

name: Hoffsess, Ruth
description: Have you seen "Rusty"? She's red-headed, and gosh, you know what goes with red hair. Well, watch her play hockey, and you'll realize that she is like other "Rustys" everywhere. Ask her who her twin is. Some day we'll see her teaching Physical Education, and maybe it won't be a whirlwind. In English class (particularly one) she's a shining star; a bright light, or something. Unfortunately she has to share her honors with "Sneaker". However, she has a habit of sharing other things such as, the Library, fudge, and Zoo notes. But for an all 'round girl, here's to "Rusty".

name: Hoyt, Dixi Crosby
motto: 'Taint 'cause he bloomin' can't, 'Tis 'cause he bloomin' won't.
clubs: Second Lieutenant, R. O. T. C. (3)
description: An apparently retiring youth with modesty and bashfulness are his basic characteristics. He's the "dark horse" from whom originated the expression, "You'd be surprised". He's a glutton for work, takes all of "Scotty's" courses, and by his untiring zeal, has worked into the hardest job in the R. O. T. C. "Dixi's" smile and hearty greeting, makes him welcome everywhere. We give him credit, in fact we envy the mortal for having such pleasing personality.

name: Hubbard, Austin Ira
motto: "Tis better to have loved and lost than never to have loved at all."
description: When the wind blew favorably from the West, Austin Ira made one gigantic leap from Walpole, and his No. 10's landed him fast in the famous Durham mud. He holds the distinction of being the only man to have ever entered college without the knowledge of the alphabet. He lived in the Barracks his first year, and was always saying, "Don't burn the barracks, fellers, I'll break the - things." He entered as an Engineer, but being disappointed in love, he transferred to the forestry division so that he could bury himself in the tall timbers, along with the rest of the wood. He thinks a woman is everything in the world - everything that he can think of.

name: Hubbard, Carroll C.
motto: "If music be the food of love, play on, Macduff; And damn'd be him that first cries 'Hold, enough'!"
description: "Care-oil" is one of our chemists. He likes the Chem. lab., possibly because it is there that he first met "Mawthy". Alas, "C.C." was a woman hater until this happened and now the Bachelor's Club is shy one more candidate. His favorite sports are beating up "Rabbit", playing bridge, ping pong, and his fiddle, and cutting class - out of the magazines.

name: Huckins, John Leslie
motto: "Another bottle of tonic, please."
description: J. Leslie, or "Ponzi" as he is better known on the campus, became disgusted with the way in which the folks up home planted "taters" so he decided to come to Durham and "learn" farming, the way Dean Taylor sees it. For three long years "Ponzi" has been trying to dope out why eggs are not square. He derived his pet nickname from his business ability in keeping the Barracks' canteen. His room, the canteen, is the library of the Barracks. Any hour of the night one can find out in which year Chaucer was born, or why co-eds leave college. But take it all in all, "Ponzi" is a real fine fellow, and his winning ways are sure to lead to success.

name: Hussey, Ivan D.
motto: Do you think they'll have it?
description: This sprightly young lad hails from up 'mongst the hills, and should one watch him as he progresses to and from his classes, he would soon realize this from his "steam-roller" stride. His Plymouth Normal School worries along with his athletic goods store, keep Ivan busy most of the time, that is, when he isn't throwing that wicked line and holding down Smith Hall door-steps. Nevertheless, he is an all 'round good scout, and is liked by everyone.

name: Jennings, Charles Winslow
motto: Perseverance is the secret of success.
description: "Win" has changed greatly since his Freshman year, for when he came to Durham women held no charm for him. He went through his Freshman year without giving the co-eds two thoughts. Things changed with the beginning of his Sophomore year. Enter a bobbed-haired Freshman. Win took the field along with a half dozen other competitors. Despite the assets of the others "Win" won the young lady's affections and he has continued to hold them ever since. But this isn't the only thing "Win" does. He usually gets sick once a year for a very good reason. However he expects to go to some warmer climate when he learns all about business, and then he won't need the sick alibi. If "Win" makes as much of a success in other things as he has in the matrimonial field, there is no doubt as to his future.

name: Joy, Mildred A.
motto: A beautiful and happy girl, Eyes glad with smiles, and brow of pearl, Shadowed by many a careless curl.
description: That ever present spark of "Joy" brightens us many a time. While the rest of us are discussing the hardships associated with the doing of a thing, "Milly" has done it without a murmur. She is not afraid of work and never shirks her duty. We always wondered why she liked Chemistry so well, but perhaps we all would if we could do as well as "Milly", and had her ability to "comprehend".

name: Kelley, Roger Milton
motto: Though college days have their delights, They can't compare with college nights.
description: "Little Nemo" came to college to get an education, but slipped on the door-steps of Congreve Hall. He says that true love never runs smoothly. When THEY say good-night, he always says, "This will be our last evening together until tomorrow night". He is sharking all his Library courses—his evening groups—and has picked for his major subjects Poli. Sci., and Phys. Ed. He is thinking of taking up Forensics and he ought to be pretty good at arguing by now. His hobby is writing letters and not sending them. "Thank you for the walk last Saturday night."

name: Kelsey, Alice Agnes
description: Alice had a Ford room-mate her Freshman year, but even if her Ford did graduate, she still runs about. She is very fond of basketball. One of her chief pastimes, besides The New Hampshire, is discussing the merits of K. U. A. Formerly Alice lived in the "Garage" at Smith Hall, but this year she has left us. Alice is a good sport and you can always rely on her.

name: Kimball, Emma M.
motto: Variety is the spice of life.
description: Emma believes in variety to the n-th degree, and gets it wherever she goes for she sure can dance. It is said that most people have some hidden secret in their lives. Emma used to have one, too, but a short while ago the cat came out of the bag and answered to the name of "Mabel", now nothing matters. She is also superstitious. Did you ever hear her sing before breakfast? Guess not! Sometimes a coincidence in names is lucky, too, "n'est-ce pas"?

name: Kimball, Rupert David
motto: Oh, ye watermeters.
clubs: President, N. H. Y. P. O. (3)
Treasurer, N. H. Y. P. O. (2)
description: "Bob" came to us from the Physics Laboratories at Washington, D. C., with aspirations to become a "hayshaker". After spending a year at Mother Nature's studies and chinning the fair co-eds, this young Lothario found that his real calling was to minister to the surrounding townspeople, and today he is one of the shining lights on our Deputation team. Within a few years we expect to see him managing an up-to-date creamery and superintending the Hopkinton Sunday School.

name: Kimball, Harold Stanley
motto: The Count of No-Account.
description: "Count" may be seen daily, handing out indigestion to his fellow-students at the Commons. In fact, it is said that he likes the place so well that he often appears at work as early as five o'clock in the morning. This incident was occasioned by an hour's advancement of his Big Ben, by some of his helpful play-mates. Harold plays admirably upon the clarinet, but his frequent trips home are ruining his left arm. Because of this fact, he will undoubtedly appear in public, as, "The One-armed Flute Player".

name: Knowlton, Dana Bowman
motto: To be idle is not hard - but does it pay?
description: When the midnight "choo-choo" left Walpole for Durham, all the girls in town, both of them, were at the station to bid "Hank" good-bye while the band was playing "Good-bye Papa's Shekels". He fell asleep on the train and rode right through to Kennebunk, Me. - but he was thinking of his girl - even today his only thought is of her - How Sweet! - when one is all he has. But he's happy just the same - the Dean told him he wouldn't have to send home for any more money - he thinks he's getting his expenses paid - I hope he's got his fare home. If ignorance is bliss, then "Hank" is the happiest man in the world. One day he's an Engineer and the next day he's an Arts and Grafter - he says he'd graduate from the Arts and Science division if they would let him major in Wood-shop.

name: Lander, Harold McKinley
motto: There's some facts in the world that don't slide along on the telegraph wires.
description: This noble young man started life catching suckers, but he lost his net in the "Tin Palace". He ought to be a cowboy because he swings a wicked line. Even though we may forget that he was a member of that famed "Tin Palace" gang we cannot forget his soulful rendering of "The End of a Perfect Day" at 2 A.M. on Smith Hall lawn. Everyone was touched; even Harold — soon after. Regarding music as a forlorn cause after a year in the band, he turned all his energies toward that forgotten engineering course and now he is an efficient expert in all branches. If you don't believe it watch him handle his slide rule. Q. E. D. higher Harold, and may your toy balloons raise you higher — still.

Even though we may forget that he was a member of that famed "Tin Palace" gang we cannot forget his soulful rendering of "The End of a Perfect Day" at 2 A.M. on Smith Hall lawn. Everyone was touched; even Harold — soon after. Regarding music as a forlorn cause after a year in the band, he turned all his energies toward that forgotten engineering course and now he is an efficient expert in all branches. If you don't believe it watch him handle his slide rule. Q. E. D. higher Harold, and may your toy balloons raise you higher — still.

name: Langdale, Edith Isabel
motto: Argument for argument's sake.
description: Upon a Home Ec. hobby's back She came to N. H. C. But now she swears by "Proffie Jack" And sews for fun you see. She loves to make cheese gingerbread, That's settled once for all, But it seems to me I've heard it said There was no cheese at all. She's proved herself a loyal pal, She's clever, thoughtful too, And although she's a "Buckeye Gal" She loves the White and Blue.

name: Langley, William Edwin
motto: Music washes away from the soul the dust of every-day life.
description: The man with the dancing feet strolled into Durham with a fiddle under one arm, and a vanity case under the other. After stopping to powder his nose and tune his fiddle, he condescended to register. "Bill" commuted his first year, but at the beginning of his second year he decided that his duties as manager of Ahern's restaurant (or was it the waitress, Bill?) necessitated his residing in Durham. If music is wanted, call "Bill". He has shown himself to be a true artist, even to his temperament. "Who took me — ?"

name: Lauriat, Frederick Thornton
motto: If hard work will bring success, 'Freddie's' career is assured.
description: Since coming to college he has made many friends among the college authorities as well as with the student body. We used to think he was quiet until this year when he surprised us one week-end by strolling about the campus with a fair young thing leaning on his arm. Whenever there is work to be done, "Freddie" is right there with a helping hand, and we are sure that there is no better help to be had.

name: Lawrence, Frederick Stanton
motto: First catch your rabbit.
description: Frederick was once but a mere child in short pants, who tipped the scale at only 120 pounds. Now he's, oh! — so much larger! He weighs 123 pounds. "Rabbit" commutes from Newmarket, but not the way Durhamites commute to Newmarket. This attraction is towards the Chemical laboratories of New Hampshire College, instead of towards the dance dens of that little town four miles away. Record has it that he has studied as long as one and a half hours at a time.

name: Mederick, Joseph Leblanc
description: "Medic" was a quiet, reserved, bashful boy, until his visits to the Commons instilled in him "the call of the wild" (Co-eds). The chase went merrily on, until a Ford Coupe appeared on the scene. This "furrow-walker" has confined his activities, of late, to the Phi Betas, and sure is going strong. In the summer months he plays at the rustic dances at Tooky Park. Here he plays the violin, cribbage, and even sings when the spirit(s) move. Mederick lost his desire for Physical Education, after wrestling with the broom and mop at Dube's Café. We wonder if he will ever forget that he has just one thing on Professor Bowler.

name: Lemieux, Leon Joseph
motto: Pete is as fat as a match, and as long as a sleigh track.
description: His winning smile greatly increased Jack Grant's trade when he worked there. He left soon after he had acquired enough cigarettes to last him the rest of his college days. "Pete's" four years at New Hampshire are only a vacation, before he starts in the carpenter's trade, under his father. Already he has been doing odd jobs around the town, in order to prepare himself for this vocation.

name: Libbey, Henry Hale
motto: The righteous eateth to the satisfying of his soul.
description: "Hen" emerged from the wilds of Reading High way back in '17 and started for N. H. For three years he wandered in the wilderness until he landed at Pettee Block, from which place he commuted to New Hampshire College for the next year. In Durham his occupation is the general study of Horticulture, but when he gets over to Dover he specializes in "Lillies". His chief ambition is to inherit a million and spend it on bananas.

name: Libbey, Anne
motto: A merry heart maketh a cheerful countenance.
description: Such a quiet, demure, little miss! When you hear anyone say this, you may be sure that they don't know Anne. Her ever present wit and good humor are a source of merriment and enjoyment to all those about her, and her favorite expression, "I didn't know what to do so I didn't do anything", always means that we will have a laugh at Anne's latest escapade. Although not athletically inclined herself, Anne is so ardent a supporter of all the college teams that she has even been known to cut class in order that she may lend her inspiration. Anne planned to teach English but she has recently developed "Wilder" ideas.

name: Lombard, Bernice May
description: Here is our Bernice so quiet, yet charming, Her face serene as the sky at dawning. And well she can yield the spoon and the roll pin For she's a member of the Home Ec. kin. That Bernice has secrets, you'd think quite real If you witnessed those epistles between Durham and Northfield.

name: Lunderville, Doris
motto: Still waters run deep.
clubs: Finance Committee, Y. W. C. A.
description: Well I asked several of my friends to compose this write-up but they were either too flattering or not enough, so "Praise to the face is open disgrace", thought I, but they are probably going on the principle, "Love thy neighbor as thyself." But I must begin. I came to college as most of the other Juniors did, and still entertain hopes of receiving a degree in 1924. My hobby is to keep my hobby a secret (what a disclosure!). Perhaps Marion Maddern would help you write the problem. Three guesses! When two girls seated alphabetically begin to amuse themselves, should the teacher use her judgment?

name: Lyford, Ruth
motto: Lyford! get that ball,
description: Can't you imagine powerful Katinka's mighty arm swinging out, —but the pale victim has escaped! Ruth just enjoys the study of modern languages—mere recreation—don't you know. Aesthetic dancing and eating peanuts are her pet hobbies. She may come from Concord, but all joking aside, she is a jolly good sport.

name: Sheridan, B. Lynch
motto: With regard to women, we do not know, He may be small, but he isn't so slow.
description: He appeared on the campus in the Fall of 1922. He has gained fatherly experience due to the protection he has extended to his room-mate "Alkey". We have watched carefully, but have never found him giving any time to the co-eds. We still have hopes though. As a future Economist he has great possibilities. Although he is small, he can hold his own with even the best of them.

name: Marion, Maddern
motto: Never do today what you can put off till tomorrow.
description: Marion's life-history began in one of the slum districts of Boston, where she distinguished herself most in the art of fancy diving. For this she has a wide and distinguished reputation. Can you beat it? For some unknown reason she selected New Hampshire with which to favor her presence. We are all glad of this because we enjoy her pleasant countenance, and we love to have her beam upon us, or perhaps act out the latest joke for us. When "Funny" first came to us, she and her chaperon walked nearly to Newmarket trying to find that town. Being used to Boston, they thought our beautiful old buildings were mere farm houses. She is a "Home wrecker" and that isn't all.

name: Margaret, Marston
high school: New Hampton Literary Institute Arts and Science
motto: First you see her then you don't.
description: When you hear the bang of doors and the clatter of feet, prepare yourself for a whirlwind, for "Peggy" is coming, eager with her latest bit of news. The big fat letters which we see her anxiously scanning each week, are the reason why "Peggy" spends her spare time making Divinity fudge. "Peggy" also finds time to be a real friend, serving those who are fortunate enough to possess this friendship.

name: Mansell, Maurice A.
motto: Breathes there a man with soul so dead, Who never to himself hath said, Oh — how I hate to get out of bed?
description: After much persuasion "Uncle Mo" decided to hibernate from the wilds of Maine, to the little hamlet known as Durham. The street cars and electric lights were too much for him, so once a year, he packs up his bag and leaves for the Canadian woods. "Mo" is no mean athlete, in that he shakes a nasty snowshoe, wields a wicked punch, tackles like a tiger, and jumps like a frog. In other words, "He's there."

name: Marston, Philip Mason
description: In the fall of 1920, "Phil" left the enterprising metropolis of Ashland, and parked his little carpet-bag in Durham. Possessed with the desire to put New Hampshire College on the map scholastically "Phil" lost no time in securing a drag with the "profs" which has succeeded in saving him the embarrassment of having to take final examinations. Up to the present time, he has been immune from feminine attractiveness but it has been said that he has broken many a fair lady's heart out in the stacks in the college library. Incidentally, "Phil" is no mean housekeeper, and some of the feeds that he has turned out for the boys have even rivaled that wonderful home cooking of the Commons. We predict that "Phil" will make some little girl a good "husband."

name: Martin, Lawrence
motto: Love will make an ass dance.
description: This beardless youth said goodbye to mamma and left Pembroke three long years ago, but he went back each week-end. Even at that "Lawrie" was the first man in the class of 1924 to bring home an N. H. Lawrence loves the women at a long distance. With the aid of Slikum and Kuppenheimer, he has made himself into a collegiate youth. "Lawrie's" only other diversions are gazing lovingly into a mirror and writing ten-page letters every day.

name: McDanols, Martha
motto: And gentle Dulness ever loves a joke.
clubs: Secretary, N. H. Y. P. O. (3)
description: Martha is a hibernating student, who occasionally forgets her aspirations when rollicking with her playmates at the Chem. Lab. where, by the way, she resides. We should think that her supply of nick-names would run out, but no, — she bequeathes each new friend with a better one and grows more proficient every day. Trips to Boston, and handiwork are both her hobbies, but who can say that she will not sometime turn into a second Thomas A. Edison and discover how to make a synthetic onion from a lemon and a carrot. We'll come to visit you when you get to hitting it rich.

name: McGlynn, Leo James
description: The portals of the "Gate City" opened wide one sunny day in September and gave up this specimen to the college. Football is his sport, and only college activity, but at this he has accomplished more than any other member of our class, namely the winning of his letter. Although he is silent about his accomplishments, in this line, there are many who are not, and they expect great things from him in the line next Fall. Shrouded under the hood of the Arts and Science division, he is fast becoming competent in the line of work he intends to follow upon graduating. Can you imagine him teaching English? His idea is to let the future take care of itself, and we are sure that it will look fondly upon him.

name: Melendy, Walter Stevens
description: Walter rattled into Durham with the "family Ford," determined to grasp all the principles of Agriculture with both hands and get all he could out of it. Having survived the class fights he decided that he was good material for the Freshman football team, and played against the Sophomores for a while. Although knowing many of the fair co-eds from Manchester, he gives them not the least bit of time. However, we do hear that each week he looks for a letter from Wakefield, Mass. He is a true friend, and is always ready to help in whatever way he can.

name: Menke, Bernhard Howard
motto: Try Zepps.
description: Here is a man who has knocked the bottom out of the theory that "no man can do two things at the same time and do them well." "Mink" has been jumping ever since he came to college. For his prowess as a high jumper he has been awarded an "N.H." The other jump was his one into society. This brought as a reward two letters, not N. H. but G. C. "Mink" says he is a busy man but outside of being on class committees, running a laundry agency, fitting shoes to the general public and taking week-end journeys to Manchester we guess his time is his own.

name: Merchant, Harriet R.
motto: A lover of the meadows and the woods, And mountains; and of all that we behold.
description: Tall and stately, this dark girl walks among us, and walks on a jeweled pathway - it's "Perley." Although Harriet has always been class A-1 in her gym work, and one of the star basketball players, she has never neglected her hiking course (Phys. Ed.-2 b., Sunday, 2.30) and leaves behind her footprints in all the highways and byways of Durham.

name: Metcalf, Daniel Messer
motto: Her eyes he thought were wonderful eyes, But he found out they were wonderful lies.
description: "Daniel in the Lion's Den" had nothing on our Daniel when he was in the "pink" of condition, and when he is thus, he plays "stationery" forward on the Varsity basketball five, absolutely refusing to move off that "nickel". On his first trip to Boston he toted a straw-suitcase, and all the "hosses" in town chased him up and down Tremont Street. His favorite dessert is apple pie and cheese. He has eyes for all the co-eds but his girl back home controls his sight. - He bought her a box of rouge last Christmas, but he got it all back again when she thanked him for it. Daniel ought to make a good business man, but he lacks the profile.

name: Morton, John Ordway
motto: If you can't get what you want; want what you get.
description: John Obadiah Morton first came to college in his father's 1909 Overland. He passed Mathematics on his profile, and developed a fondness for art. He conducted a sight touring trip to Canada one Spring, and spent the week-end with the Duke de Sherbrooke. When "Doc" Slobin's house caught on fire, John dressed himself quickly and ran for dear life. We thought he was running to help put the fire out, but later found out that he was running for some Kerosene. The co-eds and John don't hang in very well, and this is probably the reason why. He stopped one on the street one night and asked to see her home, and she told him she'd send him a picture of it.

name: Nakos, Arthur J.
motto: He's Bound to Succeed.
description: TIME WILL TELL
text:
He's Bound to Succeed.
TIME WILL TELL

name: Nesbitt, Herman Eugene
motto: Duke came to us from M. I. T. but didn't stay with us much, until his duchess came. Since then, he has been trying to maintain his dignity as a married man. He aspires to become a "Chemiker", but during exams, some of his gas gets knocked out of him. He still haunts the chem labs, but not with so much enthusiasm as before. His first castle was on the banks of Oyster River in Smith Park, but the Sunday afternoon parties were too much for his nerves. Now "Duke" is living within the city limits, but wherever he lives the castle gate stands wide open. Now don't go off mad.

name: Newell, Raymond Earl
motto: Have a haircut?
clubs: Intercollegiate Fruit Judging and Apple Packing Team (2)
First Sergeant, R. O. T. C. (2)
Second Lieutenant, R. O. T. C. (3)
description: "Ray" came to us from the little town among the hills, determined to become a Horticulturist in one year. After taking most of these courses, he decided to stay in college a while longer, and become a Zoologist on the side. He decided that life was too short to live alone so he got married. Now most of his time is spent in wielding the wicked razor and scissors for Bates the Barber. "Ray" is a mighty fine chap and deserves to succeed in his chosen career.

name: Osgood, Margaret Lillian
motto: A mind at peace with all below, A heart whose love is innocent.
description: "Margie" better known as "Peg" came to N. H. C. from Concord. She had some difficulty in selecting her major but she finally voted for English and "Doc" Richards, and by this we know that she has joined the elite who are on the high road to success. If anyone wants a favor done, "Peg" does it. She sure is a good remedy for the blues and is just big enough to forget her woes while she listens to yours. "Margie" is a pal, a good student, and a star at Dramatics. Besides this, she works hard, and is all in all, the best friend a girl could have.

"Margie" is a pal, a good student, and a star at Dramatics. Besides this, she works hard, and is all in all, the best friend a girl could have.

name: Otis, Addie Emma
motto: The blessings of her quiet life Fell on us like the dew And good thoughts where her footsteps pressed Like fairy blossoms grew.
description: When Addie first came to N. H. C., she thought she would live in Durham, but before many days, she found commuting more fun. Every day through sunshine or rain we find her faithfully attending all her classes and on time, too, when the train is not late. Although she is majoring in French, we do not know whether she is more interested in that course or in Horticulture (raising sweet "Peas").

name: Page, Gladys M.
description: Gladys is a girl who meets you half-way in everything, and when she smiles on you, you may be sure that you are there. She spends most of her time in the Home Economics Laboratory; improving her already excellent talents. She is very enthusiastic in whatever she undertakes, and is most certain to succeed in that task, but there is one thing though, she does keep you guessing about. Here's luck to her.

name: Page, Marion I.
motto: Wit, now and then, struck smartly, shows a spark.
description: Even an eventful summer at Squirrel Island did not dull the keen edge of Molly's wit. She has two big worries: her marks and her avoirdupois. In spite of these two great burdens, she always finds time for everything, and especially does not miss the social functions. By the way, have you heard where Molly leaves her earrings? In spite of a few such solecisms, she is one of those people on whom we can always rely. We all recognize Molly's Solomon-like judgment and in time of needed advice, we seek her sympathetic council.

name: Paine, Wilma M.
motto: One of the unidentical twins (?).
description: Oh, my sister's name is Florence For we are twins you see, But I ate so much and grew so fast I'm three years older than she. From her quiet attitude, you would never guess that Willie could tell a joke; but she can, good ones at that. If you don't believe it, ask her roommate. Aside from being quite a humorist, she is quite a student. What more do you want?

name: Paine, Henry Harrison
motto: There is no fun in a graveyard — give me my flowers now.
description: "Mickey" Paine, the old B. A. A. runner, has an alabaster brow (1% softer than ivory). This was the cause for his entering as a two-year "Aggie". He went out with a co-ed one night and when he returned, he went up to his room and tipped a bottle to his lips. "Home-brew?" asks "Ted". "Nope! paint remover," answered "Harry". But even so, this youth with the build of a man and the brain of a child, has proved to be one of the greatest track men to ever attend New Hampshire. He went to Belgium with the Navy Olympic Team, and since his return to this country he has been showing us his speed on the cinder path.

name: Pascoe, Thomas E.
motto: He's a woman's man.
description: Northeastern didn't compare with New Hampshire State in Pascoe's mind, so last fall he thought it advisable to register with the class of 1924. Tea rooms and fair damsels play a big part in this man's life, and along with twelve hours of stiff (?) courses, he feels that he is getting one hundred percent from his college career. We are hoping that within a year or so, the Government will see fit to map out the wild and woolly portions of New Hampshire, so we can find from whence this mountaineer hails.

name: Parkhurst, Wayne
motto: He's little, but oh my!
description: This specimen of the wilds of Colebrook blew into Durham in the fall of 1920, with the firm determination to become famous, and with a sweeping glance at the Greek letter fraternities after his name, we are all agreed that he is fast reaching his goal. But studies are not the only thing in Wayne's estimation. His many week-end trips to W——, make even the most unassuming, suspicious. In spite of these few failings, he is well liked by everyone with whom he comes in contact.

name: Pennell, Rachel Florence
motto: And thereupon the quene.
clubs: Financial Committee, Y. W. C. A. (3)
description: And it came to pass that there entered these famous halls of learning one co-ed yclept Rachel. And she did live at ye Commons, and she did instruct the celebrated dwellers of that house, in the mysteries of the noble and revered game of Truth. And in time they did hail her leader of ye gang, and loud praises did they sing to her honor, and of her clothing, much did they borrow. And e'en forsooth it came to pass that she did learn to crave for music, the breath of the Gods, and she did listen to the bass horn of the orchestra. But, alack, that was many years ago.

name: Phillips, Herbert
motto: A blithesome brother at the bowl, A welcome guest in hall and tower; He knows each place where wine is good 'Twixt New Castle and Hollywood.
description: Way back in the year of 1920, a weary tow-head straggled in from the wilds of Littleton. After sleeping for a couple of weeks, "Hubby" started a Zoo in the "Tin Palace". He soon found it necessary to make frequent trips to Worcester for very important data (?). "Hubby" is one of the fearless fire-fighters of Durham. He likes to see the little red wagon tear along. The fire-truck even calls for him before going to the fire. Over there, "Hubby" was able to duck machine-gun bullets, but there are two twinkling eyes on the campus that have made a direct hit. We wish you luck, old man. "It's time for me to retire."

name: Phillips, William Stanley
motto: When you dance, I wish you were a wave o' the sea, That you might ever do nothing but that.
description: Is it just a coincidence that "Bill" came from Marblehead? Kind of tough on the town, eh? We have it through good sources, that "Bill" spent the summer in New York. To the scoffers, he showed his sun-burned tonsils as a proof. The only difference between Rudolf Vasselino and "Bill" is that "Bill" is unknown to the world at large. He dances very exquisitely, furthermore, he admits it. He is a good scout, has the faculty of making friends and keeping them. What better tribute can be named?

name: Pichette, Charles Francis
motto: I say, just what I think, and nothing more nor less.
description: Ye Gods! Why do all Chemists have that worried, sour look? Urged on by the hectic record of his noble brother, Charlie shut both eyes and plunged into the Chem. course. But the King furnished an eye opener when he passed out the marks. In spite of natural and supernatural handicaps, Charlie has become a good Chemist. His talent in the musical line is both vocal and instrumental. As a singer, well (?), but he is a wonderful violinist. He formerly relied upon the co-ed to pick dancing partners for him, but those days have gone forever.

name: Pingree, Ruth E.
description: Perhaps you've never met her But she's been here three whole years And the longer she's among us The more her worth endears. She's ambitious, but so modest That you never, never know All the things that she's accomplished That make us admire her so. The nice things that I could say Are really without end, But the nicest of them all is She's the truest kind of friend.

name: Pratt, Harold Arthur
description: Instead of following in his father's footsteps, "Pratty" decided to follow his brothers, so he packed his bag and grabbed off a B. & M. train to the village of Durham with the idea that he was to become a Chemist. After seeing so many engineers wearing glasses "Pratty" decided to join the "Arts and Grafters" after the first term. He says the reason for this was that he wanted to give a certain "Jane" some time. "Pratty" pushes a mean telegraph key and occasionally assumes control of the local station when he isn't doping out the Freshman baseball schedule.

name: Price, Frank Walter
high school: Amesbury High School; Mount Hermon School
motto: Oh, what an oil-can you turned out to be, Since you drifted away from the class of '23.
description: In his early College days, some fair damsel captured his Fraternity pin, and he was forced to sacrifice one whole year to recover it. During this period, he was Police Court reporter for the Boston Telegram, but he soon found that he was too big a man for the paper game, so he returned to College. It is an undisputed fact that Frank has the best judgment of any man on the campus. He ought to have, he never used it any.

name: Putney, Charles Henry
motto: I didn't do it.
description: He is what one might call a lover of nature, with birds being his favorite. He is always glad when Easter comes so that he may see his Robins. As far as we know he is a co-ed hater. Maybe he is afraid that a little bird will fly North and whisper in a certain one's ear. At any rate he is never seen with one on the Campus. Studies seem to play but a small part in his life, but movies and sports with interior decorating (?), seem to take the greatest share of his time.

name: Redmond, William P.
motto: My only looks are woman's looks And jollies all they taught me. — Anon.
description: He came to us a confirmed woman hater and successfully resisted the snares of the co-eds for a whole year. History repeated itself, however, and he fell before Alexander's Conquest. He still exists in his state of subjection. He keeps some wonderful notebooks until they are lost in the labyrinths of Smith Hall. His willingness to help is known to more than one co-ed. He is one of those rare men to whom a friendship is sacred. His criticism is always constructive and he is never too busy or tired to help a friend. I am not going to call up Smith Hall again this term.

name: Richards, Sarah C.
motto: Are you ready, brothers?
description: "Gr-r-r! Get out of here and let me sleep!" One shuts the door just in time to avoid a volley of pillows, sweaters, and other missiles from Sarah's bed. For Sarah is vehement, yea, even unto the manner in which she upholds her sentiments in the Debating class. She once dreamed of becoming an author, but the rapidity with which her manuscripts were sent back from The Ladies' Home Journal, and Good Housekeeping makes us wonder if she had not better try Snappy Stories or possibly The Dial. Sarah returned from her brother's wedding a changed woman, and now spends her time reading Vermont News, and is looking forward to the date of her sorority dance.

name: Roberts, Stanley Byron
motto: There was a young student named, 'Stan' A Harvard and Yale football fan, He goes to Smith College For to gain all his knowledge And for the co-eds he don't give a ——.
description: "Stan" represents a most remarkable hybrid type, a combination "Arts and Grafter" and "Aggie". How does he get that way? Well he suddenly found it more economical to raise those posies himself, to keep his little "Turpentine" supplied. But "Pa" says, "Burbank doesn't need to worry yet awhile."

name: Rogers, Olive Mae
motto: Raven locks and big brown eyes.
description: Once upon a time in 1920, a Hudson drove up in front of Smith Hall and stopped, depositing the contents which consisted of the aforesaid "Olivia". Since that time she has divided her time between The New Hampshire, the Library, and the Y. W. C. A. We are wondering whether anything or anyone else ever occupies her time and attention. Olive has the reputation of being a sound sleeper, but this good reputation was questioned when she lived on the ground floor of Congreve Hall. Ask her if she is afraid of burglars. Besides having a good disposition, Olive is one of the best of friends.

name: Merton W. Rowe
motto: A beardless youth is but half a man.
clubs: First Sergeant, R. O. T. C. (3)
description: Have you ever noticed how smooth "Strangler's" hair is? Someone drained the oil out of Stowell's Ford and he found it on "Strangler's" bureau. This explains the mystery. Wrestling isn't a major sport, but "Strangler" lost his frat pin by it. He has but one "Holt". "Strangler" planned to transfer to Dartmouth until he went in for wrestling. Although not an "Aggie", he has that typical barnyard stride. Besides wrestling, his other major sports are football and dancing.

name: Maurice James Sargent
description: When "Sarge's" town got too small for him he had to move or suffer, so he came to the even smaller town of Durham. Owing to a natural fear of the canine, he was forced to park his junk for the first two years in a private house. He shows his industrial inclination by slaying wood for Dean French's fireless cooker. He spends the rest of his spare time studying, and doing other small jobs around the practice house. "Sarg" has never been seen with a woman yet. Apparently he believes in fate. Anyway "Sarg" is a good scout, says little, thinks much, works more, and it is expected that some co-ed will fall for him soon.
