name: Blanche Eliza Sawyer
motto: A merry heart doeth good like a medicine.
description: We wonder what Milford did after giving up so much of cheer, unselfishness, and ready sympathy as is contained in the little girl loaned to New Hampshire College for awhile. Cheerfully helping others, most often at a personal sacrifice which she seems to disregard, is most typical of her. She thinks that men are divided into two classes: "batches," and the other -- well they don't count (not much).

motto: God made him, therefore let him pass for a man.
clubs: First Sergeant, R. O. T. C. (2)
description: John was dissatisfied with the class of '23, so he jumped into the class of 1924. John admits that he has been around some, in that he has been in Durham and the far East, Andover and other parts of the United States. He may be a good singer (?). He has legs like a canary, and a head like a cuckoo. John is also a track man, his course being the track to Dover. As an actor, "Bull" Montana has nothing on John, but after all he is one of the gang.

name: Shand, Wesley Bruce
motto: Jimminy Christmas.
description: After blowing into Durham, from the little town of Manchester, "Wes" promptly placed himself under the guidance of "Pa" Taylor, made up his bed in Ballard Hall, and signed up for Glee Club. Only a year's experience and the influence of "Red" Richardson, were necessary to convince him that his calling was "chickuns." When not at The New Hampshire office, or at the Poultry Plant, he spends his time editing THE 1924 GRANITE, and raving about his future farm up 'mongst the hills.

name: Sheldrick, Helen M.
motto: Plough deep, while sluggards sleep.
description: Helen came to college with higher hopes of becoming a History "shark", and has kept up this ambition for two years. But in her third year she decided that she would rather spend her Saturday evenings at the movies and her Sunday afternoons walking than in the "Lib" doing outside reading in History. She studies hard, belongs to the Glee Club and we wish her all kinds of success in her dramatics.

name: Smith, Alfred Willard
motto: Sentimentally, I am disposed to harmony.
description: "Smitty" is one of the most musical (?) members of the class. To fully appreciate his ability, one has only to be present when he is composing one of his rhapsodies on the piano. You will either praise him or kill him. Still "Al" is a business man and if any of his money-making schemes ever materialize, he will land either on Wall Street or in jail.

name: Smith, Mary Blanche
motto: Joyous as the morning, Thou art laughing and scorning.
description: "Smithie" surely has changed in appearance if in no other way since she has come to N. H. C. Quite contrary to the laws of what ought to be, she entered New Hampshire a grown-up person with hair piled high on her head; but she will leave looking quite a few years younger because of her "bob." She is popular because of that same "bob," because of "pep", because of her wit, and lastly because of her curling iron. She is very fond of making new resolutions and so far, they haven't lasted long; but then, variety isn't always the spice of life.

name: Smith, Rodney Perkins
motto: Shave and a hair cut, two bits.
description: Since the front door yard of Plymouth Normal offered no further inducements to remain at home, "Tweet" sought new specimens which brought him to N. H. C. First love lingers, which no doubt explains the reason for sixteen-page letters and a box of fudge per week. It was through the agency of Mr. Smith that we first met Marthie (?). He gets much ado about nothing when questions are asked. If chemistry becomes a dead science he can return to his old business, manufacturing post holes for the Telephone Co. name: Smith, William Watson
motto: Ain't nature careless.
description: "Bill" is a hard hitter. He is practicing with gloves every day to pound into shape another map like his own. He is very kind-hearted in that he likes to give better than to receive. With the aid of his friends, "Bill" is going through school as an honor student. Because of his nocturnal solos, he now sleeps in the Dean's well-house. "Bill" loves not woman, but woman kind, not much but many.

name: Snow, Thomas Leonard
description: "Tom" broke into the College limelight by having his picture taken with the Orchestra. In those days, he was playing second fiddle, but he is now playing first fiddle—as far as he knows. "Tommy" is a student, in the eyes of his parents; no warnings have ever reached Claremont. — (He works at the Post Office.)

name: Steeves, Reginald Van Tassell
motto: "You can always tell a Freshman, But you cannot tell him much."
description: Reginald "Corntassell" Steeves, saxophonist and bootlegger combined, (never one without the other), drew out a Mil. Art suit and has been wearing the spirals ever since. His dentist told him that he had a large cavity which needed filling, so he started in college—and his dentist was right. When he isn't arguing with Adams, he is focussing his attention on the cue ball and the others—we hope he gets a pillow for a birthday present so he can sleep on the pool table too, just to make the days complete. His interest in the co-ed is very vague—we understand that a co-ed gave him an awful look one day—we don't know whether she gave it to him or not, but, he sure has got one.

name: Sterling, Ruth H.
description: Ruth is the shyest and the tiniest of us all, She's scarcely any larger than a great big china doll, But even tho she's tiny, she certainly can work And she never yet was known, her duty to shirk. She has patience, and courage, and lots of pluck Which so far has brought her all kinds of good luck. There is one thing about her we cannot understand Why every week-end regularly, she doth leave our merry band. "To go and see her folks", that is Ruthie's plea; This we cannot question for we never went to see. "Ruthie" is the sort who ne'er forgets a friend And is always ready a helping hand to lend. She has but one fault, which isn't bad at all It's just to become big, and nice, and tall.

name: Stevens, Elsie
high school: Laconia High School; St. Mary's School
motto: Better faithful than famous.
description: Elsie tripped the light fantastic into Durham in the Spring of 1921, registering as a special, but through her concentrated efforts in studying, she is now a full-fledged Junior. She sure is an all 'round artist for she can draw men as well as pictures. But sometimes the wires get crossed, and sparks fly, though "L.C." always comes out smiling, and looks for more mischief to let her artistic temperament indulge in.

name: Stevens, Richard Don
motto: How many inches to a foot?
description: Since Gadbois has graduated, "Mud" is the official owner of the "dirty shirt" of the Varsity Squads. Why anyone who holds such a distinction doesn't give the co-eds any time remains a mystery. "Mud" has a brother who is a professor in the Forestry Department, so he decided that his only salvation was to take the Forestry course. He also cooks for his brother's gang in the good old summer time. He has been out for all of the Varsity teams, and is only waiting until they make bull-fighting a major sport, hoping to come through as a dark horse, and win a letter.

name: Stewart, Morris A.
motto: Lightly from fair to fair he flew, And loved to plead, lament, and sue.
description: "Stewie" rolled into Durham in a little broken-down "flivver." Evidence points that he has since remained badly bent. He developed several bad habits during his Freshman year, one of which has cost his father a large sum of money in providing him with glasses looking for the right co-ed. He now wears rubber tired ones to absorb the shocks. In another year the census taker in Durham can add a few more names to his list. "Doc" will have left and all the neighborhood cats will be safe, for he has turned away from his medical pursuits and has taken up Entomology. When he leaves this institution of learning, he will carry our best wishes with him. "I'm off the women."

name: Stone, Rena M.
motto: Here's to Rena who's friendly to all, Always ready at duty's call, If you want something done, she'll do it for you And do it gladly and cheerfully, too. She likes to work, she likes to play And can keep a secret for many a day, After all of this is said To this conclusion we are led, We are glad we know Rena Stone If it's just for friendship's sake alone.

name: Stowell, Samuel
motto: If hot air were music, he'd be a brass band.
clubs: Individual Prize Winner, Winter Carnival of 1922
description: Did Stowell ever try to convince you that you wanted to buy a thing when you knew more about it than he? If so you will have to admit that he sure does carry a big gas bag. "Sam" was selling second hand Fords and hot water bottles last summer. But he couldn't convince people in this section that he knew what he was talking about, so he obtained a job digging out for the foundation of the new theater. Sometimes "Sam" has visions of stepping out but that is as far as he ever goes.

name: Studley, Robert A.
motto: And when a lady's in the case, You know all other things give place.
description: "Stud" did not find college life a new experience due to the fact that he had previously attended Massachusetts Agricultural College. He first registered "Aggie", but later found his sphere in the English department. He belongs to the "Legion of Married Men", and when he is not in the classroom, he may be seen entertaining, or perhaps being entertained by his charming young daughter. "Stud" is quiet in his ways, but his friends soon learned to listen to his suggestions and wait expectantly for his questions. He is one of the few men in college who realizes what he is here for, and this combined with his love for efficiency, should insure success.

name: Summerville, Hazel Mary
motto: She speaks, behaves, and acts just as she ought.
description: "Hazie" said, "Yes, you may do my write-up if you promise not to mention Lawton's name". So we won't say a word about him. But we enjoy those chocolates that come from some mysterious source almost as much as those boxes from home. We'll say that Hazel's mother is some cook. When there's any fun going on, "Hazie" is right there, and the fact that she is a member of Book and Scroll, shows that she studies, too. Well, we're here to tell you that she can write "some" poem and she's going to make the best English teacher that the Queen City ever saw.

name: Thompson, Marjorie E.
motto: She may be thin, and look all in, But you never can tell.
description: Nobody has anything on "Marj" yet. She certainly is in a class by herself. You can't miss that Southern drawl, and when she gets excited she talks slower than ever. Her determination could shake "T" Hall itself. Her hobbies are tennis, and snowshoeing, and also Sunday dinner parties.

name: Tucker, Harris Wiggin
motto: A Jack of All Trades.
description: The master of all trades, a decorator, dancer, sign-painter and songster, breezed into town with the best of intentions but the question (To "B" or not to "B") presented itself to Harris. Rumor has it that the "bee" stung "Trixie" and he ran away. Since this episode he has devoted his energies to making his "W" (Wellesley Club) and passing a few of the college courses. Many of the office doors which bear the trademarks of the "profs" are a result of "Trixie's" brush, and we wonder if some of those 90's "Tuck" drags down have anything to do with this?

name: Voyagis, Michael
description: "Mike" started in his career by sharking the two-year Agricultural Courses. Now he is taking up Chemistry for the pleasure of assisting the "stenog." over at Nesmith Hall. Most of his first year was spent in hunting for Coleoptera in Pettee Block. He became interested in insects and the following fall, elected a course under Prof. O'Kane in order to find some means of taming them for the Zoo. He also derived much pleasure from walking out into the country with the girls, putting kerosene on the waters to drive away the mosquitoes from Durham. We all know that "Mike" is a good pal, and a loyal friend, and feel sure that he will become famous as a Chemist in years to come.

name: Wadleigh, Ruth
motto: Silence is golden.
description: Of all Congreve's top floor gang, Ruth is the proctor's pet. She surely is a case with her witty tongue and laughing face. We hate to call her a grind, but that she is when Marion Maddern and she begin to cram. Remember the night when you had more than a barrel of fun, "Waddles?" In spite of her youthful appearance, she has been loyal to the "Home Ec-ers" these three years and will soon be an experienced teacher.

name: Walker, Frank Arthur
motto: Unlike Midas' famous trait, Everything he touches is bound to break.
description: Frank's favorite expression is to "Bo-gash". This must be the pass-word of either the Manchester Club or the "Dirty Dozen". He holds a charter membership in both of these organizations. He has a weakness for the fair sex, but rather than shatter any fair one's idealistic dreams, we will knock off here.

name: Welcome, Arthur L.
motto: Spread it thin.
description: This aspiring youth from the town of "cigarette fame" came to Durham College with the idea of showing 'em what they raise in the Connecticut River Valley. His record excels all others, in that he has completely demolished three spreaders, and although in the firm clutches of Prof. Richardson, he still raves about "his woman". However, "Warkum" bids fair to shine in the chicken world, and we all wish him the best of success.

name: Weston, John Prentiss
motto: Agricultural
description: "Punk" Weston came to N. H. College in the Fall of 1918 as a modest young man who had never hit the high spots in his home town except at the Bennington Fair with the pride and joy of one of Bennington's 400. He spent two years of his four years' loaf consuming the Midnight Mazda preparing for an Agricultural career. At this point, "Punk" decided to put theory into the practical, and so it was "back to nature" for young Punk. On his return to college, "Punk" quickly laid aside his overalls and boots, to don the "Klassey Kollege Klothes" for Men of Character, and learned to smoke Dromedaries for Men Who Think; and he could shake a hoof that would make Salome look like a Bohunk at the "Annual Fireman's Ball". In short, Punk became "collegiated."

name: Warren, Whitcomb
motto: I've got just one bad habit.
description: This individual personage hails from the "Unique" metropolis of Bath; unus for one, equus for horse, One Horse Town. He started to blaze his trail three weeks before college opened and managed to arrive here for registration day. He began his noble career in the historical "Tin Palace" where he learned his A, B, C's of Beeculture — d. "Whit" does not attend many social functions, but as far as functions are concerned, he has many. He has many friends, including his standby "ips", namely "Chip", "Drip" and "Lips."

name: White, Emma Louise
motto: To the quiet belong the spoils.
description: Emma has won many friends by her winning smiles and she is ever ready to help those in trouble. She starts for her 10:00 o'clock classes at 10:15, but still she gets there. How about that Peerless car, Emma?

name: Whittemore, Ruth Caroline
motto: I've had my writing criticized before.
description: "Rufus" came down to New Hampshire College with the intention of becoming a "Home-Ec-er", but it conflicted so much with her wrecking ability that she dropped from its ranks and drifted into Prof. Babcock's History classes. Perhaps she intends to be a great Historian some day, we don't know, but we surely think that by this time she must have amassed a ponderous amount of knowledge from the long hours of intense (?) application at the Library. You will have to go a long way to find a more conscientious, faithful girl, or a more true friend. We know that she will succeed, because she will put into everything she does, the same amount of untiring energy that she has in her work at N. H. C.

name: Wilkinson, Charles Henry
motto: Many a good man marries a widow.
description: "Charlie" winters in Durham and summers at Madbury Beach, where he is taking up light housekeeping. "Charlie" started out to buy a Stutz, but is now content to ride in his friend's Ford coupe. His ambition is to become a Poultry husbandman so he has picked up a hen, and now expects to enter the chicken industry. Charles is a great salesman, and we believe that he could sell lingerie to Dean Pettee. As an actor, he has "Ben" Turpin stopped.

name: Williams, Pricilla A.
motto: Oh! Wait a minute.
description: We admit that English and Mathematics is a queer combination. However, Pricilla should be famous if for no other reason than that she can place all the quotations that Dr. Richards gives out. She goes home every week-end to see her brother, but then there are a lot of brothers in Exeter.

name: Woodin, Ernest N.
motto: Where's my pipe, I've got to smoke.
description: Although this Hollis husky spent two years building ships for "Uncle Sam", he decided that his calling was not metal-working, so he decided to come to New Hampshire State to study the modern methods of farming. When he isn't rubbing tobacco for his next smoke or dolling-up for an informal, he spends his time prepping for the coming classes or reading the Red Book monthly.

name: Young, Adaline R.
motto: Old friend, kind friend! Never be thy shadow less, Never fail thy cheerfulness.
description: Although Adaline commutes part of the time, we shall never forget her room in Smith Hall, known as the "Home of the Commuters." Upon first acquaintance, she seems sedate, but her good sportsmanship and intellectual ability cannot be concealed. Chemistry is her one delight, while Physics stands not far behind. Once a friend, always a friend. And as to preference, we do not know, but we think it is an A T Ω.

motto: Where girls are, Eddie isn't.
description: "Eddie" is the tutor on the side lines in all his classes. Whenever the boys get stuck in their lessons, they always run to him for assistance. Edward Hale's from Madbury, commuting to and from that city. He says that if its population were 29 instead of 28, he would make a jitney out of his Buick and run a line between Durham and Madbury. "Ned" is taking the Electrical Engineering course, fitting himself to be a farmer. Nevertheless he's got a secret all his own - sh-h - he's goin' ta be "Guv'ner uv Noo Humpshire", some day. Never mind "Eddie", in whatever you do, we wish you luck.
